atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports back pain on his right side lasting approximately one week.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports movement down to his right lower side.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports seeing blood in his urine.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient describes the pain as constant.

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient mentions feeling slightly nauseated.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician is prescribing ibuprofen 800 mg as needed for pain.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician is ordering a urine culture.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician is ordering a BMP.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician is ordering a strainer for the patient to monitor urine output.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician is scheduling a follow-up appointment in approximately one to two weeks.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician recommends increasing fluid intake to facilitate stone passage.

atomic_propositions.speaker: unknown
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: A kidney stone in the right ureter is the primary problem.

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: Physical exam reveals mild tenderness to palpation in the abdomen, particularly near the flank on the right.
